across.clue: to make someone or something seem less valuable or respected
to show that you are happy and proud at your own success or at someone else's failure.
An ability to know or understand something through your feelings rather than by considering facts or evidence.
to praise someone in order to get something you want especially in a way that is not sincere.
Very small or very young — used humorously to describe someone, especially a child, at an earlier time in their life | knee-high to a ___
To officially stop something from being done especially by making it illegal
a short or temporary period when you fail or forget to do things in the right way.

down.clue: Make someone aware of something | Bring something to someone's ___
Prepare yourself mentally or physically | ___ yourself
At the most active, busy, or energetic stage; fully underway and operating at peak level | in ___ swing
Someone who uses force to take and keep power in a country
to measure or describe something as a quantity.
To apply or direct force, influence, skill, or pressure on something in order to achieve a result | ___ to bear
someone who tends to concentrate on their own thoughts and feelings rather than communicating with other people
To give someone the freedom to do what they want for example by taking them out of a situation in which their behaviour is controlled
the happiness you feel when you have everything you want and you enjoy your life.
A burden or source of guilt that constantly weighs on you and is difficult or impossible to escape | an ___ around your neck
the loss of the ability to move your body or a part of it usually because of an injury or illness.
